A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR WAYNESVILLE IN WARREN COUNTY OHIO AND CONTINUED SOUTHEAST INTO CLINTON COUNTY TO NEAR WILMINGTON. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED EF1 WITH MAX WINDS OF 105 MPH. THIS TORNADO WAS PREVIOUSLY LISTED AS TWO SEPARATE TORNADOES, HOWEVER ADDITIONAL INFORMATION HAS LED TO THE CONCLUSION THAT A SINGLE TORNADO OCCURRED FROM NEAR WAYNESVILLE TO NEAR WILMINGTON.

TREE DAMAGE AT BUSH CEMETARY WAS EXTENSIVE, AND CONSISTED OF BOTH SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES THROWN IN MULTIPLE DIRECTIONS. A WELL-DEFINED SWIRL WAS NOTED IN THE CORNFIELD TO THE SOUTHEAST OF BUSH CEMETARY.

TWO HOMES SUFFERED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. ONE OF THE HOMES SUFFERED PARTIAL UPLIFT OF THE ROOF AND A SIGNIFICANT LOSS OF THE COVERING OF THE ROOF. THE GARAGE WAS ALSO HEAVILY DAMAGED AND THE FRONT PORCH WAS LIFTED. SPRUCE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND LARGE LIMBS FROM OTHER TREES WERE BROKEN. THE RESIDENCE LOCATED ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF U.S. ROUTE 22 HAD SHINGLES AND SIDING REMOVED. AN UN-ATTACHED ONE CAR GARAGE WAS DESTROYED.
